reasoning: The Federal Reserve has generally been in a rate-cutting cycle or holding steady in late 2024 and 2025 according to market expectations. With the 4-week rate at 4.020% in mid-October 2025, further rate cuts are expected by early 2026 (the question close date). Consequently, it is unlikely that the 4-week treasury bill rate will have increased over the 2025-10-26 value by early 2026. Short-term bursts are possible but the general trajectory is flat to downward. I estimate roughly a 20% chance of an increase, reflecting possible surprises in inflation causing the Fed to hike rates or treasury yields to bounce up.
